seats.name: Mathabhanga
seats.note: Nisith Pramanik — BJP Union Minister (MoS Home Affairs)

seats.name: Chopra
seats.note: Hamidul Rahaman — largest TMC margin in North Bengal (69,124)

seats.name: Sujapur
seats.note: INC wins Sujapur — one of only two INC seats statewide

seats.name: Rejinagar
seats.note: Humayun Kabir (AJUP) — independent regional bloc wins Rejinagar by 58,876

seats.name: Naoda
seats.note: Humayun Kabir (AJUP) — same candidate wins second seat (Naoda)

seats.name: Krishnanagar Uttar
seats.note: Tarak Nath Chatterjee — highest BJP margin in Nadia (78,361)

seats.name: Habra
seats.note: Debdas Mondal — BJP wins Habra (margin 31,462)

seats.name: Kamarhati
seats.note: Madan Mitra — TMC holds Kamarhati narrowly (5,102 margin)

seats.name: Rajarhat New Town
seats.note: Piyush Kanodia — narrowest margin in state (316 votes)

seats.name: Sandeshkhali
seats.note: Sanat Sardar — BJP flips Sandeshkhali ST; site of 2024 protests

seats.name: Hingalganj
seats.note: Rekha Patra — BJP wins Hingalganj; she was Sandeshkhali protest face

seats.name: Canning Purba
seats.note: Saokat Molla — TMC wins Canning Purba by 54,231 (South 24P highest)

seats.name: Baruipur Paschim
seats.note: Biman Banerjee — Assembly Speaker retains Baruipur Paschim

seats.name: Diamond Harbour
seats.note: Pannalal Halder — TMC wins Diamond Harbour (Abhishek Banerjee's former HQ)

seats.name: Bhangar
seats.note: Md Nawsad Siddique — ISF holds Bhangar, only ISF seat statewide

seats.name: Behala Paschim
seats.note: Dr. Indranil Khan — BJP wins Behala Paschim (margin 4,201); Partha Chatterjee's old seat

seats.name: Bhabanipur
seats.note: Suvendu Adhikari — BJP wins Bhabanipur; Leader of Opposition retains seat

seats.name: Singur
seats.note: Arup Kumar Das — BJP flips Singur; Becharam Manna loses

seats.name: Balagarh
seats.note: Sumana Sarkar — BJP wins Balagarh by 41,914 (Hooghly highest)

seats.name: Goghat
seats.note: Prashanta Digar — BJP wins Goghat SC by 49,582

seats.name: Haldia
seats.note: Pradip Kumar Bijali — BJP wins Haldia by 49,062

seats.name: Nandigram
seats.note: Suvendu Adhikari — BJP retains Nandigram by 35,441 (LoP stronghold)

seats.name: Keshpur
seats.note: Seuli Saha — TMC wins Keshpur by 50,105 (Paschim Medinipur highest)

seats.name: Taldangra
seats.note: Niladri Sekhar Dana — wins both Taldangra (11,332) and Bankura (54,177)

seats.name: Bankura
seats.note: Niladri Sekhar Dana — wins Bankura by 54,177 (2nd highest margin)

seats.name: Hansan
seats.note: Milton Rashid (INC) — one of only two INC wins statewide
